skill.name: emil-design-eng
skill.description: Design engineering guidance for UI polish, motion decisions, component details, and frontend interaction reviews.
skill.summary: Apply detailed UI polish and animation guidance to frontend interfaces.
skill.author: emilkowalski

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples, inline code, and prose in SKILL.md. The only confirmed risk is low severity: hardcoded external resource links that do not send data or execute code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported 133 Ruby or shell backtick executions. The cited SKILL.md content is Markdown guidance and CSS or JavaScript examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. No command invocation or user-controlled shell input was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only scanned file is Markdown, and the cited locations are tables or fenced examples. No executable script file or runtime loader exists in the file structure.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is a Textual False Positive
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography matches occur in metadata, headings, prose, or UI examples. No cryptographic API, hashing operation, cipher configuration, or credential handling was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context is natural language and UI animation advice. I found no evidence of cryptographic implementation or use of weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Resource Links
low_findings.description: SKILL.md includes links to design and easing resources. These links are static references and do not transmit local data, but they may send users to external websites.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links to educational resources. They are a confirmed network reference, but there is no automated request or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern Is a Wording False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged reconnaissance terms in instructional prose and UI review examples. No command reads system state, enumerates files, collects host data, or reports environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited file is static instructional content. I found no executable reconnaissance command or data collection path in the skill.

content.value_statement: Many interfaces work correctly but still feel slow, rough, or generic. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code detailed guidance for polished motion and component reviews.
actual_capabilities: Reviews UI code using a required Before, After, and Why table format.
Recommends animation timing, easing, and transform-origin improvements.
Explains when animations should be removed, reduced, or made instant.
Provides CSS, React, Framer Motion, and Web Animations API guidance.
Checks accessibility concerns such as reduced motion and touch hover behavior.
Identifies performance issues in animation properties and JavaScript-driven motion.
limitations: It reflects one design philosophy and may not fit every product style.
It does not run automated visual tests or performance traces by itself.
It focuses on interaction polish, not full brand or product strategy.
Some recommendations require browser support checks before production use.

use_cases.title: Refine a Component Library
use_cases.description: Review buttons, popovers, tooltips, tabs, and toasts for polished motion and responsive interaction details.

use_cases.title: Audit Product Interface Motion
use_cases.description: Find animations that feel slow, unnecessary, inaccessible, or inconsistent with the product tone.

use_cases.title: Improve Documentation Examples
use_cases.description: Turn rough UI examples into clearer component guidance with better defaults and edge case coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Review Basic UI Polish
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review for a single component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this button, menu, or tooltip for UI polish. Use a Before, After, and Why table with concise recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Animation Timing
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate motion tuning for existing UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these interface animations. Recommend durations, easing curves, transform origins, and cases where motion should be removed.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Interaction Edge Cases
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this component for hover, active, keyboard, touch, reduced motion, loading, and dismissal behavior. Prioritize issues by user impact.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Polished Component System
prompt_templates.prompt: Help define motion and interaction rules for a frontend component system. Include defaults, exceptions, accessibility rules, and review criteria.

output_examples.input: Review a dropdown animation that uses a slow ease-in transition from zero scale.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the sluggish entrance, recommends a faster ease-out curve, starts from a visible scale, and explains the perceived speed impact.

output_examples.input: Audit a toast component with keyframe entry animations and swipe dismissal.
output_examples.output: The response recommends interruptible transitions, matching enter and exit direction, pointer capture, velocity-based dismissal, and reduced motion support.

output_examples.input: Improve a documentation site example for a polished tab control.
output_examples.output: The response proposes clearer active-state transitions, shorter stagger timing, touch-safe hover behavior, and examples that users can inspect quickly.

best_practices: Ask for the component code, expected interaction frequency, and product context before tuning motion.
Use the table review format so recommendations stay specific, comparable, and easy to apply.
Validate motion on real devices and include reduced motion behavior in final guidance.
anti_patterns: Do not apply decorative animation to high-frequency keyboard workflows.
Do not use the skill as a substitute for accessibility testing or browser profiling.
Do not treat every recommendation as universal when the product tone requires restraint.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps review and improve UI motion, component polish, interaction details, and frontend animation choices.

faq.question: Does it write production-ready UI code?
faq.answer: It can suggest implementation details, but teams should test browser support, accessibility, and performance before release.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this only for animations?
faq.answer: No. It also covers component defaults, interaction edge cases, performance, touch behavior, and documentation examples.

faq.question: Can it replace a product designer?
faq.answer: No. It provides design engineering guidance, but product goals and brand decisions still need human ownership.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk low?
faq.answer: The command and cryptography alerts were false positives in Markdown content. Only static external links remain as a minor concern.
